text.title: Mobile Perf Budget Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every mobile-perf budget must enforce. These rules cover the three axes that define mobile experience: launch speed, animation smoothness, install footprint.

rule.statement: Budget covers three axes: cold_start_time, frame_drop_rate, binary_size. Reject budgets with fewer axes; missing any axis leaves a class of regressions undetected.
rule.rationale: Cold start (Time-to-Interactive), frame drops (jank), and binary size (download abandonment) are independent failure modes. A "fast" app that is 200MB still gets uninstalled.

rule.statement: Measure each axis BOTH on real devices in lab (deterministic baseline) AND via RUM in production (distribution). Reject budgets enforced via emulator/simulator results alone.
rule.rationale: Emulators consistently over-perform vs real devices. RUM-only misses regressions in slow networks / low-end devices. Both signals are required.

rule.statement: CI runs the perf gate on every PR. Merge is BLOCKED if any axis budget is exceeded. Reject manual override without engineering manager + perf owner co-sign.
rule.rationale: Without CI gate, budgets decay within 90 days. CI is the only enforcement that survives team turnover.

rule.statement: When a budget breaches, attribute the regression to a specific PR within the window (compare bisect / git blame). Reject "we'll figure it out later" — attribution is part of the failure response.
rule.rationale: Attribution within 24h converts at 80%; past 1 week, drops to 30%. Fast attribution preserves the fix.

rule.statement: Budgets include a low-end-device floor: iOS = iPhone SE 2nd gen OR equivalent A12-class chip; Android = mid-tier device with ≤ 4GB RAM. Reject budgets measured only on flagship devices.
rule.rationale: User-base mobile distribution skews low-end in many markets. Flagship-only budgets miss 30-60% of real-user experience.

example.title: perf-budget.yaml with all rules met
example.code: platform: ios cold_start_p50_ms: 1200 # measured on iPhone SE 2nd gen cold_start_p90_ms: 2400 frame_drop_pct_per_screen: feed_scroll: 0.3 modal_animation: 0.0 binary_size_mb_max: 65 measurement: lab_devices: [iPhone SE 2nd gen, iPhone 15] rum_provider: firebase_performance ci: gate_branch: main block_on_exceed: true override_requires: [eng_manager, perf_owner] last_regression: date: 2026-04-12 pr: 1842 axis: cold_start_p90 resolution: deferred-launch-screen-network-call
example.note: Illustrates r1-r5: 3 axes, lab + RUM, CI block, regression history, low-end device included.

example.title: Flagship-only synthetic budget
example.code:
cold_start_ms: 800 # measured on iPhone 15 Pro Max only, simulator binary_size: no limit frame drops: unmeasured CI gate: none, manual review
Result: user reports of 8-second cold start on low-end devices. No regression history. Team doesn't know which PR caused it.
example.note: Illustrates r1-r5 violations simultaneously. Common pattern in teams without perf discipline.
